data.title: Aker BP reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Aker BP press release (OTCQX:AKRBF): Q1 oil and gas production reached 441 thousand barrels of oil equivalent per day (mboepd) during the quarter.
  • The full-year guidance remains unchanged at 390-420 mboepd. 
  • Production cost amounted to $6.5 per barrel.
  • Net profit of $316 million and cash flow from operations of $2.1 billion for the quarter.

data.title: Icahn Enterprises Q1 bottom-line loss balloons on slumping investments
data.description:
Icahn Enterprises (NASDAQ:IEP) posted a hefty loss on its investing activities in Q1, dragging its bottom line further into the red, the company helmed by famed activist investor Carl Icahn disclosed on Wednesday. 

The company recorded a net loss from investment activities of $394M, compared with a $103M loss in Q4 2024 and a $96M loss in last year's Q1. The Q/Q decline was mostly due to $224M of losses in its investment segment, driven by losses in the healthcare sector, the company said.

Its indicative net asset value was $3.00B at March 31, 20256, vs. $3.34B at Dec. 31, 2024, mainly reflecting a decline in NAV of the holding company's interest in investment funds to $2.48B from $2.70B. Lower NAV at its Viskase subsidiary — to $159M from $197M — also contributed.

Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.79 fell from -$0.19 in the prior quarter and -$0.09 a year ago.

Q1 net revenue of $1.87B slid from $2.56B in Q4 2024 and $2.47B in last year's Q1.

Total expenses of $2.52B vs. $2.69B in Q4 and $2.49B in Q1 2024.

Adjusted EBITDA attributable to IEP (NASDAQ:IEP) was -$287M vs. $12M in the previous quarter and $134M a year ago.

Icahn Enterprises (IEP) stock slipped 0.3% in premarket trading.

data.title: Nature's Miracle announces up to $20M financing with GHS Investment
data.description:
• Nature's Miracle (OTC:NMHI) said on Wednesday that the company has  entered into an equity purchase financing agreement with GHS Investments. 
  • The company can require that the investor invest up to $20M to purchase the company's shares of common stock, $0.0001 par value per share, over the course of twenty-four months immediately following the effective date of the registration statement to be filed with the SEC. 
  • The company issued 1.5M shares of common stock to the investor in consideration for the financing.  In addition, the investor made a $250,000 investment in the company's Series A shares of preferred stock, which are convertible into shares of common stock at a conversion price of $0.112 per share.

data.title: Lumentum in focus as Needham praises after strong Q3 results, guidance
data.description:
• Lumentum Holdings (NASDAQ:LITE) was in focus on Wednesday as investment firm Needham praised the communications equipment maker after it reported stronger-than-expected quarterly results and guidance.
  • Shares rose more than 6% in premarket trading.
  • “3Q was driven by continued strength in Telecom demand from Cloud operators including DCI, while Datacom benefited from another record in EML lasers,” Needham analyst Ryan Koontz wrote in a note to clients. “4Q is expected to see similar trends with a surge in 800G Datacom modules for its lead customer, Google. Importantly, 4Q [gross margins] should improve despite the headwind from XCVR modules and tariffs. EML fab and Thailand production capacity expansions and new module customer wins remain on track to drive strong F26 growth as [management] reiterated its $500MM bogey exiting [calendar 2025].”
  • Koontz continued: “[Management] believes it can avoid costly China tariffs and is hopeful U.S. policies will relax regarding Thailand. While acknowledging tariff risks, we raise our F25/F26 [estimates] and reduce our PT to $100 on broad multiple compression in Tech.”

data.title: OSI Systems secures $36M contract for aviation security systems
data.description:
• OSI Systems (NASDAQ:OSIS) said on Wednesday that its Security division has received a contract award for approximately $36M to deploy and service airport screening solutions for a prominent international airport in the Middle East. 
  • Among the systems to be delivered are the Orion 920CT checkpoint screening system integrated with Rapiscan TRS for inspecting carry-on items, Orion 935DX for screening air cargo and pallets, and Itemiser 5X for detecting trace amounts of explosives and narcotics. 
  • The company is also expected to provide ongoing maintenance and support throughout the product lifecycle.
  • OSI Systems (NASDAQ:OSIS) shares were up more than 2% in premarket trading.

data.title: Asia Pacific Wire & Cable appoints Ben Lee as CFO
data.description:
• Asia Pacific Wire & Cable (NASDAQ:APWC) announced on Wednesday that Ben Lee will join the company as chief financial officer (CFO), effective May 12, 2025.
  • Prior to joining APWC, Lee served as CFO of O’Neil Global Advisors. He previously held VP Finance roles at Yum! Brands, Shanghai; and Canadian Solar.
  • Shares of APWC were down -6.21% to $1.51 during premarket trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: LightPath Technologies secures $4.9M order for infrared cameras with existing defense customer
data.description:
• LightPath Technologies (NASDAQ:LPTH) on Wednesday announced its subsidiary G5 Infrared, a leading high-end infrared camera systems manufacturer, received a $4.9 million follow-on order from an existing defense industry customer.
  • "Delivery of the order will take place throughout our 2026 fiscal year and in addition, we expect to receive additional orders for this defense customer's program in the future," said LightPath Chief Executive Officer Sam Rubin.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Elanco spikes after Q1 beat and guidance raise
data.description:
Shares of Elanco Animal Health (NYSE:ELAN) traded higher in the premarket on Wednesday as the veterinary product maker raised its full-year revenue outlook after exceeding Street forecasts with its Q1 2025 financials.

The Greenfield, Indiana-headquartered drugmaker posted $1.1B in revenue for Q1, beating the consensus by $20M despite a ~1% YoY drop on a reported basis amid an unfavorable impact from the divestiture of its aqua business.

While ELAN’s Pet Health revenue dropped ~1% YoY to $635M against a backdrop of a challenging U.S. retail environment and falling vaccine sales in the U.S., its Farm Animal revenue slumped ~2% YoY to $546M due mainly to unfavorable impacts from the aqua spinoff and forex rates.

However, ELAN’s adjusted net income for Q1 reached $184M, indicating a ~9% YoY rise and exceeding the consensus by $0.07 per share, while its adjusted EBITDA fell ~6% YoY to $276M, driven by a 130 basis-point drop in EBITDA margin from the prior-year period.

Reaffirming its 2025 outlook for adjusted EBITDA and EPS, the company raised its revenue guidance to $4.51B - $4.58B from $4.445B - $4.510B previously and ahead of $4.5B projected by analysts, citing a $65M - $70M tailwind related to favorable foreign exchange rates.

However, ELAN’s Q2 outlook for revenue at $1.175B - $1.195B was in line with the consensus of $1.18B, while EPS guidance of $0.17 - $0.21 fell short of the $0.25 projected by analysts amid $5M attributed to unfavorable impact from foreign exchange rates.

data.title: LifeStance Health Group GAAP EPS of $0.00, revenue of $333M
data.description:
• LifeStance Health Group press release (NASDAQ:LFST): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.00.
  • Revenue of $333M (+10.9% Y/Y).
  • For full year 2025, reiterating expectations for revenue of $1.40 billion to $1.44 billion, Center Margin of $440 million to $464 million, and Adjusted EBITDA of $130 million to $150 million.

data.title: Siemens Healthineers AG reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Siemens Healthineers AG press release (OTCPK:SEMHF): Q2 Adjusted basic earnings per share rose slightly to €0.56 from €0.55 in the prior-year period. Higher earnings contributions from the operating business were impacted by lower net financial income as well as higher tax expenses.
  • Revenue rose by 6.8% in the second quarter of fiscal year 2025 to around €5.9 billion. The Imaging and Varian segments, in particular, contributed to this strong revenue growth.
